sources_exist.note: All eleven URL occurrences resolved to accessible pages.

figures_match_sources.note: Five audits fail claim-level support: the consolidator count uses a broader-than-PE scope, the buy multiple is a range midpoint rather than an observed median, the adoption citation omits the embedded-use statistic, the historical page supplies no elapsed adoption period, and the pricing page omits the claimed 44% contractor share.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Role fractions, pi_dist and pi_moat are bounded and broadly plausible for a physical licensed trade, but t50_years=4 uses an asserted 15-20-year FSM diffusion history absent from its citation and blends 25% any-current-AI use with a separately claimed 12% embedded-material-use measure.

rubric_consistent.note: Deterministic inputs and arithmetic exactly match, the heterogeneous-code treatment is explicit, and PE evidence is confined to B/M. However, active_consolidators counts institutional/public strategic platforms beyond the requested PE-backed scope, and buy_mult uses the midpoint of a broad advisory range rather than a median actually paid.

cross_checks_honest.note: All four cross-checks are present and the no-proxy and terminal-value discussions are candid, but pricing_model_deflation states a 44% flat-rate share that the cited pricing page does not contain.

source_audits.note: The page repeatedly presents an 18-platform verified list for 2024-2026, but its inclusion criteria admit public-company strategics and other institutional-capital vehicles, not only PE-backed add-on platforms; it also inconsistently states 27 in the header and 19 in later FAQ text.

The page supplies advisory ranges of 4x-8x EBITDA for residential add-ons and 4x-7x for $1M-$5M EBITDA firms, but no distribution or observed transaction median establishing 6.0x as the median actually paid after owner-comp normalization.

The release supports a third-party survey of 1,000 residential contractors, 74% viewing AI as an efficiency engine, and about 25% currently using AI. It does not report the quoted companion figures of 12% with embedded AI or 34% experimenting, even though those figures are used to distinguish material adoption in t50_years.

The page states 7x-12x EBITDA for platform-quality residential HVAC firms and documents approximately 17x Redwood and 18.5x Champions recapitalization anchors, supporting 12x as an upper-end $10M+ platform assumption.

The secondary compilation states 51% adoption for 100-999 employee companies and 29% for firms under 100 employees, but gives no commercial-availability start date or evidence that either segment took 15-20 years to reach those levels.

The page says the average U.S. small-business owner is nearly 60, more than half are over 55, plumbing ownership skews older, and succession options are limited. It supports the stated basis for the explicitly labeled 30% estimate, though it does not directly measure a plumbing/HVAC 60+ share.

The contractor price-book vendor explains fixed upfront flat-rate pricing and its profitability/efficiency incentives, supporting the rationale that short-run administrative savings need not pass through hourly billing.
The guide distinguishes time-and-materials from fixed flat-rate pricing and states that flat-rate efficiency can yield more jobs and profit, supporting the pricing-mechanism portion of pi_dist.
EPA's Section 608 page describes refrigerant-service prohibitions, requirements, and technician-certification programs, directly supporting a federal refrigerant-handling barrier to DIY bypass.
The valuation guide describes varying state HVAC license regimes and license-holder concentration/transfer risk, supporting the state-licensing component of the moat rationale.

The page says more than 92% of homeowners prefer flat-rate pricing but contains no 44% contractor-adoption figure and does not establish the record's residential replacement, commercial cost-plus-ish, or membership-revenue mix claims.

reasons: inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/private-equity-hvac-2026/) uses an internally inconsistent tracker whose 18-platform inclusion scope includes public strategics and other institutional vehicles; re-count only PE-backed platforms with qualifying HVAC/plumbing add-ons in the required 24-month window.

inputs.buy_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/private-equity-hvac-2026/) provides broad advisory ranges, not a median actually paid; cite observed transactions or a dataset that establishes the normalized small-firm EBITDA median.

inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.servicetitan.com/press/servicetitan-report-finds-74-of-residential-contractors-see-ai-as-key) supports about 25% using any AI but not the 12% embedded-material-use statistic used in t50_years; cite the companion study directly and align the selected adoption definition and population to material operational use.

inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://fieldservicesoftware.io/blog/field-service-management-software-statistics/) reports current size-tier adoption percentages but no 15-20-year diffusion interval; provide dated evidence for both the analog's start and approximately 50% adoption point before deriving t50_years.

inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://profitrhino.com/flat-rate-the-pricing-method-contractors-and-homeowners-prefer/) does not report the claimed 44% company share or the broader segment billing mix; provide a survey or primary benchmark covering contractor pricing-model shares and separately source commercial/new-construction and maintenance-agreement mix.
